name: Comparison Split
description: Editorial two-column comparison table framed as a long-form decision aid. An optional project meta strip (source / window / verified-by) anchors the section above the table; rows can be grouped under mono-caps section headers and each row carries an optional clarifying note; the preferred (right) column is visually elevated with a primary-tinted band and a 'Recommended' eyebrow. Below the table, a methodology footnote, a pull-quote, an outcome metrics band, and a closing CTA let the section carry an entire decision page on its own.

descriptions.descriptive: The table renders as a 1.6/1/1 grid (collapsing tighter on mobile) inside a rounded outer border. The header row carries a 'Criterion' mono-caps label on the left, the two option labels with subtext lines in the middle, and on the right a primary-rule top border plus an 'Option B · Recommended' eyebrow that signals the preferred path. Body rows alternate base-100 and base-200/40 stripes; boolean values render as filled check icons (primary tint) or muted cross icons inside soft circles, while string values render inline at body weight. Optional row groups insert a base-200 band carrying a mono-caps section header. Above the table, a 4-up project meta strip frames the comparison as a single engagement; below it, an optional methodology footnote, pull-quote, dark metrics band and CTA close the case. Every micro-label uses font-mono with 0.22em tracking; headings are text-balanced and scale up to text-6xl on desktop. Reduced-motion users get a static fallback; everyone else gets a 0.08s staggered fade-up reveal.
descriptions.usage: Use whenever a decision benefits from a side-by-side breakdown with editorial depth: before/after rebuilds, plan tier comparisons, DIY-vs-service framings, head-to-head competitor positioning. The optional meta strip, row groups, methodology footnote, pull-quote, metrics band and CTA let the section stand in for a full comparison page. Place after a hero or below a stats panel; never duplicate it on the same page with another comparison table or pricing-tier grid covering the same axis.
descriptions.audienceFit: Established B2B companies, SaaS platforms, professional services firms, and any team selling against a legacy incumbent. Resonates with corporate buyers in finance, technology and consulting who treat transparency and methodology footnotes as buying signals, and with any audience that wants to skim a decision matrix without reading marketing copy first.
